name: Empedraillo Trail
name_original: Salto del Fraile desde Miranda del Rey - circular trailrunning por el parque natural Despeñaperros
description: Jogging the moderate 5.1-mile Empedraillo Trail in Despeñaperros Natural Park offers varied terrain and the beautiful Salto del Fraile Water
region_intro: Jogging routes around Santa Elena traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, from dense woodlands to open areas. The region offers a mix of elevations, providing options for different fitness levels. Runners can expect paths that wind through natural settings, offering a connection to the local environment. The area's physical features include rolling hills and established trails, making it suitable for outdoor running activities. Best jogging routes around Santa Elena The most popular jogging route is Empedraillo Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of surfaces and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and expansive views. Local runners also love the Running loop from Santa Elena, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through local surroundings, often completed in about 25 minutes. Jogging around Santa Elena is defined by established trails, varied elevations, and natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Santa Elena?
A: The running routes around Santa Elena feature varied terrain, from dense woodlands to more open areas. You'll encounter rolling hills and established trails, offering a mix of surfaces and elevations suitable for different fitness levels. The region is characterized by its natural landscapes, providing an immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers routes suitable for beginners. For an easier option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.4 miles (3.9 km) long and typically takes about 25 minutes to complete. This route leads through local surroundings with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some of the more challenging running trails in the Santa Elena area?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Santa Elena has several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 23.2 km and features substantial climbs.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Santa Elena?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Santa Elena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to La Aliseda – Llano de las Américas loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or natural attractions along the running trails?
A: Yes, the Santa Elena area is rich in natural beauty and offers several viewpoints and attractions. You might encounter stunning vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;. There are also beautiful waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural monuments can I discover while running in Santa Elena?
A: Beyond viewpoints and waterfalls, the region around Santa Elena features interesting natural monuments. You can explore sites like the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Batán&lt;/a&gt;, which add a touch of history to the natural landscapes.

Q: How many running routes are available in Santa Elena?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Santa Elena listed in this guide. These routes vary in difficulty and length, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santa Elena?
A: The running trails in Santa Elena are popular among the komoot community, with over 100 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often praise the established trails, diverse elevations, and the opportunity to connect with the natural environment.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes for those looking for a balanced challenge?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989758766/empedraillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empedraillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 5.1-mile (8.3 km) route typically takes about 1 hour and 4 minutes to complete and features a good mix of surfaces and elevation changes, offering a balanced workout.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails in Santa Elena?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, Santa Elena is a town, and local transport options may exist. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or taxi services for access to trail starting points, especially for routes further from the town center.

Q: Are there options for longer distance running in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy longer runs, Santa Elena offers several extended routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 23.2 km (14.4 miles) trail, providing a significant distance challenge with considerable elevation gain.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Empedraillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The Empedraillo Trail is approximately 8.3 kilometers (5.1 miles) long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 4 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and the varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on the Empedraillo Trail is varied and can be quite demanding. You can expect sections with stony or cobblestone paths (empedrado), as well as sand and riverbeds. These uneven surfaces require good ankle strength and stability, making it a challenging and dynamic experience for joggers.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, including stony paths and riverbeds, the Empedraillo Trail might be challenging for absolute beginners. Joggers with some experience and good physical condition will likely find it more enjoyable. It's designed for those looking for a dynamic and engaging route.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight along the Empedraillo Trail is the beautiful Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring. The trail also offers panoramic views, especially if it follows a river, providing scenic natural beauty throughout your jog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Empedraillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog the Empedraillo Trail depends on the local climate. Generally, spring and autumn offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for outdoor activities like jogging. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, potentially wet conditions, making the varied terrain more challenging.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and how do I get there?
faqs.answer: The Empedraillo Trail is located within the Despeñaperros Natural Park. While specific trailhead parking details are not provided, access to natural parks often involves designated parking areas near popular routes. Public transport options might be limited, so driving is often the most convenient way to reach the park and its trailheads.

faqs.question: Is the Empedraillo Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the Empedraillo Trail is located within Despeñaperros Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Despeñaperros Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for general access to Despeñaperros Natural Park for activities like jogging on established trails. However, specific activities or guided tours might require prior arrangement. It's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or special requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer:
Yes, the Empedraillo Trail intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the

Empedraillo (99%), Transandalus (69%), Las Asperillas (48%), and Camino del Hornillo (46%) trails. Other shorter overlaps include Molino del Batán, Los Estrechos de Miranda, Antigua Vía Romana, and Mesa del Rey.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and potentially challenging terrain, it's recommended to bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip and ankle support. Carry enough water, especially on warmer days, and consider bringing snacks. A small first-aid kit, sun protection, and a fully charged phone are also advisable for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within Despeñaperros Natural Park, amenities directly at the trailhead might be limited to basic facilities like parking or informational signs. For cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you would likely need to visit nearby towns or villages surrounding the natural park.
